pubmed_links: [Williamson et al. (2014) Heterozygous loss-of-function mutations in YAP1 cause both isolated and syndromic optic fissure closure defects](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24462371/);[Fan et al. (2023) The transcriptional co-activator Yap1 promotes adult hippocampal neural stem cell activation](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37083045/);[Chen et al. (2024) YAP and TAZ differentially regulate postnatal cortical progenitor proliferation and astrocyte differentiation](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38639242/)
summary: Heterozygous loss-of-function variants in YAP1 cause a recognized syndrome (uveal coloboma-cleft lip and palate-intellectual disability) featuring ocular coloboma, orofacial clefting, and intellectual disability; the Hippo/YAP pathway also regulates adult hippocampal neural stem cell activation and postnatal cortical progenitor proliferation. (emerging evidence)
